project.name: jobsync
project.description: JobSync is a free, open-source, self-hosted web app for managing the job search journey — tracking and organizing job applications locally, with EURES/ESCO integration for European job discovery. Note: this project has over 100 source files; consider scoping analysis to a subdirectory for faster results.

nodes.name: referral.actions.ts
nodes.summary: Referral aggregate Repository for the Inside Track feature: records insider/network tips, drives the status state machine, and reifies referrals into Jobs.
nodes.languageNotes: DDD Repository pattern; status transitions validated via isValidReferralTransition; every Prisma query is userId-scoped per ADR-015.

nodes.name: assertUsablePerson
nodes.summary: Guard verifying a Person belongs to the user and is not consent-blocked (GDPR Art. 7(3)).

nodes.name: recordInsiderTip
nodes.summary: Records an insider referral tip, creating a Referral linked to the tipster and target company.

nodes.name: recordNetworkTip
nodes.summary: Records a network referral tip enforcing the connects-tipster-to-insider network-path invariant.

nodes.name: transitionReferral
nodes.summary: Drives a referral through its status state machine, validating each transition.

nodes.name: commitReferralToApply
nodes.summary: Reifies a referral into a Job application (TipReifiesToJob), creating the Job and linking provenance.

nodes.name: getReferral
nodes.summary: Fetches a single referral with related person/company data, owner-scoped.

nodes.name: listReferrals
nodes.summary: Lists referrals for the user with optional status/kind filtering.

nodes.name: 026-multi-channel-notification-architecture.md

nodes.name: 031-health-escalation-via-degradation-event.md

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with the README to understand what JobSync is: a self-hosted, open-source job-application tracker built on Next.js 15, Prisma/SQLite, and Shadcn UI, with EURES/ESCO European job discovery, AI resume review, and a CRM. This document frames the product's purpose and major features, giving you the vocabulary for the rest of the tour. The architecture overview doc then zooms one level in, laying out the layered system context and the key architectural decisions you'll encounter as you read the code.

tour.title: App Entry & Routing
tour.description: JobSync runs on the Next.js App Router, so the runtime entry points are the layout and page files under src/app. The root layout wraps every request in the locale, theme, and error-capture providers, while the root page redirects users to the dashboard or sign-in based on auth state. The dashboard layout then composes the Header, Sidebar, and global undo/activity listeners that frame every authenticated screen — this is the shell the whole UI lives inside.

tour.title: Authentication & Server Bootstrap
tour.description: Building on the routing shell from Step 2, this step shows how requests are secured and how server-side singletons come alive. src/auth.ts wires NextAuth's Credentials provider with bcrypt verification and enriches the session, while the edge middleware sanitizes credential params and applies security headers on every request. instrumentation.ts is the Next.js startup hook that boots the scheduler, event consumers, holiday pre-warm, and other globalThis singletons you'll meet later in the tour.

tour.title: The Job Aggregate & Repository Pattern
tour.description: JobSync follows Domain-Driven Design: each aggregate has one domain model plus one server-action file acting as its Repository. job.model.ts defines the core Job vocabulary (salary periods, status, contact-role enums, validators), and job.actions.ts is the largest action surface, handling job CRUD, status transitions, notes, and tags. Every action returns the ActionResult<T> value object — the typed response contract used consistently across the whole application layer.
tour.languageLesson: ActionResult<T> is a discriminated union: a success branch carries data: T, an error branch carries a message. TypeScript narrows the type after you check the discriminant, so callers get compile-time safety on which fields exist.

tour.title: The Data Layer — Prisma & DB Access
tour.description: Underneath every Repository sits the data layer. prisma/schema.prisma is the authoritative data model defining all SQLite tables, relations, and indexes, and db.ts exposes the single Prisma client singleton (cached on globalThis to survive hot-reload) that all server actions import. Understanding this pair grounds the domain models from Step 4 in concrete persistence — the Job model maps directly to tables declared here.
tour.languageLesson: Prisma migrations are immutable, ordered SQL files. Each migration applies an incremental schema change, letting the database evolve alongside the code; the schema.prisma file is the desired-state source of truth the migrations build toward.

tour.title: The Connector / Module ACL
tour.description: JobSync's most important architectural pattern is the App ↔ Connector ↔ Module Anti-Corruption Layer (ADR-004). manifest.ts defines the shared kernel — the ModuleManifest types every external integration self-describes with — and registry.ts is the unified ModuleRegistry singleton (one of the highest fan-in files in the codebase, depended on by 40+ modules). register-all.ts is the side-effect barrel that pulls every module into the registry at startup. This layer keeps foreign API shapes from leaking into the domain.

tour.title: A Module in Action — Job Discovery Runner
tour.description: Now see the Connector pattern run end-to-end. The job-discovery runner orchestrates a full automation: it resolves credentials (PUSH pattern), calls a module's search, dedups against the blacklist, enriches, matches, and stages results. The EURES module is a concrete DataSourceConnector that translates foreign EURES vacancy shapes into the domain's DiscoveredVacancy type, while automation.actions.ts is the Repository that lets users configure and trigger these runs.

tour.title: The Event Bus
tour.description: Aggregates and connectors communicate through a TypedEventBus rather than calling each other directly, keeping bounded contexts decoupled. event-types.ts declares the domain-event type enum and the createEvent typed factory, event-bus.ts implements the globalThis publish/subscribe singleton with runtime payload validation, and index.ts is the public barrel consumers import. When the runner from Step 7 stages a vacancy or promotes a job, it publishes events here.
tour.languageLesson: Event payloads are linked to Zod schemas via `satisfies z.ZodType<XPayload>`, so adding a field to the TypeScript interface without updating the schema (or vice versa) is a compile error — the type system enforces that runtime validation stays in sync with the static contract.

tour.title: Multi-Channel Notifications
tour.description: A major consumer of the event bus is the notification system. types.ts defines the NotificationChannel interface and DispatchContext, the ChannelRouter fans a draft out to every enabled channel (in-app, email, webhook, push), and dispatch-context.ts consolidates 11-13 per-dispatch DB queries into six parallel reads built once per cycle. This is the late-binding pattern: events trigger notifications that re-localize at render time, connecting back to the i18n system in Step 12.

tour.title: The Scheduler & Run Coordination
tour.description: Automations don't run themselves — the scheduler cron loop (bootstrapped by instrumentation.ts in Step 3) finds due automations and dispatches them. Critically, every run, scheduled or manual, must pass through the RunCoordinator: a globalThis singleton enforcing a per-automation mutex to prevent double-execution, with watchdog, progress reporting, and degradation bridging. This ties together the runner from Step 7 and the events from Step 8 into a coordinated execution pipeline.

tour.title: CRM Core & The Inside-Track Referral Lifecycle
tour.description: Person is an independent CRM aggregate root, not part of the Job aggregate. person.model.ts holds its value-object parsers (emails, phones, companies, social) and state machine, while person.actions.ts is its Repository including GDPR-sensitive merge, anonymize, and consent withdrawal. The Inside-Track layer builds on top: insideTrack.model.ts defines referral and connection vocabulary, and referral.actions.ts records insider/network tips and drives the referral lifecycle — the project's most recent feature wave.

tour.title: The Inside-Track Data Model
tour.description: Grounding Step 11 in persistence, the welle5_inside_track migration adds the tables behind the referral feature. The Referral table tracks inside-track job referrals with tipster, target company, and forwarded state; PersonConnection models a directed relationship between two CRM persons with a kind. Seeing the SQL alongside the domain code shows how the Person aggregate's relationships map onto foreign keys at the database level.
tour.languageLesson: Foreign keys in these migration tables enforce the directed Person→Person and Referral→Company relationships at the database level, so the application-layer invariants in person.model.ts are backed by referential integrity in SQLite.

tour.title: Internationalization (i18n)
tour.description: Every user-visible string in JobSync is translated across four locales (EN, DE, FR, ES). i18n/index.ts is the single most depended-upon file in the codebase (181 fan-in) — the client adapter barrel exposing useTranslations and the LocaleProvider — while server.ts is the server-only counterpart and dictionaries.ts merges all namespace dictionaries. The adapter pattern means consumers import only from these barrels, so the underlying dictionary system can be swapped without touching feature code.

tour.title: Containerization, Orchestration & CI
tour.description: As a capstone, see how JobSync ships. The Dockerfile is a multi-stage build (base → deps → builder → runner) producing a minimal Node 20 Alpine image, docker-compose.yml orchestrates the app service with its data volume, and the GitHub Actions CI workflow installs dependencies, type-checks, and runs the test suite on every push. Together these package everything you've toured — the App Router, server actions, connectors, and scheduler — into a deployable, continuously-verified system.
tour.languageLesson: Multi-stage Docker builds use multiple FROM stages: the builder stage carries dev dependencies and compiles the app, while the final runner stage copies only the production artifacts, cutting image size dramatically. Layer ordering (dependencies before app code) maximizes build-cache reuse.
